---
title: "Zak Hynd"
---

Geospatial data scientist working across remote sensing, geostatistics, and spatial Python. The background: a decade building geoscience software as a product person. A Master's in Geospatial Data Science brings the technical depth forward.

The product work took competing demands from different business units and turned them into things a development team could build against: a feature-priority framework for a geomodelling platform used across industries, a customer feedback loop run across most of the decade, a process for getting working prototypes in front of users before release. The same instinct shows in [how I work with AI](projects/ai-workflow.qmd) now – tasks divided deliberately, intermediate output checked, the LLM directed rather than deferred to.

Geology underlies all of it. Fieldwork, data wrangling, processing, structural interpretation – that's the domain knowledge the SAR work draws on now. [River braid mapping](projects/river-braids.qmd) is the clearest current case: Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical fused to track river-channel change after the 2021 Canterbury flood, built as a proof of concept for catchment-management workflows.

Before any of this, geology and zoology at Otago, and a touch of Iceland. Christchurch-based; flexible from there.

[See all projects →](projects.qmd)
